natural_language: A retailer decides a single-period order quantity for one product. Demand is 111. Unit ordering cost is 1, holding cost for leftover inventory is 1, and shortage penalty is 9. Formulate and solve a linear optimization model.

A retailer decides a single-period order quantity for one product. Demand is 61. Unit ordering cost is 2, holding cost for leftover inventory is 2, and shortage penalty is 6. Formulate and solve a linear optimization model.

A retailer decides a single-period order quantity for one product. Demand is 116. Unit ordering cost is 5, holding cost for leftover inventory is 1, and shortage penalty is 14. Formulate and solve a linear optimization model.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 6 periods. Initial inventory is 1. Period demands are [16, 20, 28, 29, 47, 53]. Unit ordering cost is 1 and holding cost is 2. Use inventory balance constraints to minimize total ordering and holding cost.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 6 periods. Initial inventory is 7. Period demands are [43, 52, 32, 15, 25, 59]. Unit ordering cost is 4 and holding cost is 3. Use inventory balance constraints to minimize total ordering and holding cost.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 5 periods. Initial inventory is 4. Period demands are [28, 36, 21, 20, 39]. Unit ordering cost is 1 and holding cost is 3. Use inventory balance constraints to minimize total ordering and holding cost.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 5 periods with backorders allowed. Initial inventory is 19. Period demands are [36, 22, 66, 49, 54]. Unit ordering cost is 1, holding cost is 4, and shortage penalty is 9. Formulate a linear model with inventory and shortage variables.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 5 periods with backorders allowed. Initial inventory is 20. Period demands are [59, 43, 56, 32, 65]. Unit ordering cost is 1, holding cost is 1, and shortage penalty is 18. Formulate a linear model with inventory and shortage variables.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 4 periods with backorders allowed. Initial inventory is 9. Period demands are [25, 34, 26, 44]. Unit ordering cost is 3, holding cost is 4, and shortage penalty is 18. Formulate a linear model with inventory and shortage variables.

A warehouse replenishes 2 items over 4 periods. Initial inventories are [14, 12]. Demand matrix by item and period is [[33, 32, 23, 27], [14, 48, 50, 20]]. Item volumes are [2, 2] and storage capacity per period is 114. Minimize ordering and holding costs subject to inventory balance and capacity constraints.

A warehouse replenishes 2 items over 3 periods. Initial inventories are [20, 15]. Demand matrix by item and period is [[30, 35, 27], [14, 23, 46]]. Item volumes are [3, 2] and storage capacity per period is 130. Minimize ordering and holding costs subject to inventory balance and capacity constraints.

A warehouse replenishes 4 items over 3 periods. Initial inventories are [20, 5, 13, 19]. Demand matrix by item and period is [[45, 44, 26], [47, 37, 47], [35, 33, 24], [18, 42, 41]]. Item volumes are [1, 1, 1, 2] and storage capacity per period is 88. Minimize ordering and holding costs subject to inventory balance and capacity constraints.

A firm plans replenishment over 5 periods with fixed ordering cost. Initial inventory is 0. Demands are [22, 33, 42, 25, 44]. Unit ordering cost is 3, holding cost is 2, fixed ordering cost is 52. Use binary order trigger variables and Big-M constraints with M = 166.

A firm plans replenishment over 3 periods with fixed ordering cost. Initial inventory is 16. Demands are [55, 34, 55]. Unit ordering cost is 5, holding cost is 2, fixed ordering cost is 29. Use binary order trigger variables and Big-M constraints with M = 144.

A firm plans replenishment over 5 periods with fixed ordering cost. Initial inventory is 10. Demands are [25, 49, 48, 15, 53]. Unit ordering cost is 4, holding cost is 1, fixed ordering cost is 27. Use binary order trigger variables and Big-M constraints with M = 190.
